mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (p-value > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or shows negative correlation in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflect supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, restrictive monetary policy increases discount rates and reduces corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or yields negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad economic expansion and higher corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no positive correlation with 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or produce negative returns in > 50% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-shock recovery phases signals synchronized supply-side rebound and capacity utilization improvement, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.
